content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
April 14, 1989 
Judith Sproul Davis 
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson, 
Parrinello & Mueller 
770 L Street, suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Ms. Davis: 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-203 
We have received your letter of April 5, 1989 seeking written 
confirmation of the advice I provided in our telephone 
conversation of March 30, 1989. 
This is to confirm that your 
letter accurately reflects that advice. 
If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5901. 
DMG:JGM:ld 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General counsel~ 
~J~n~~an 
Counsel, Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Roy R07 •

SAN FRANCISCO 
650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650 
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108 
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800 
LAW OFFICES OF 
NIELSEN, MERKSAMER, 
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER 
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS 
770 L STREET, SUITE 800 
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752 
FILE NUMBER 
5066.02 
April 5, 1989 
Mr. John McLean, Counsel 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J street, suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear John: 
Re: 
Advice Regarding contributions From PAC 
Restricted Funds to the project 90 Committee 
For Debt Reduction 
This is to confirm my understanding of the advice you 
rendered during our telephone conversation on March 30, 1989. 
I 
called you because several of our political action committee 
clients have been informed that "restricted" funds could be used 
to contribute to the "Project 90" committee for purposes of 
retiring that committee's debt to a bank. 
A copy of your letter 
to Assembly Minority Leader Ross Johnson (dated March 23, 1989) 
was provided as authority for this. 
During our conversation, you stated that although your 
letter to Mr. Johnson was not fact-specific, you had advised him 
that a non-controlled PAC could contribute from restricted funds 
to the Project 90 committee so that Project 90 could payoff a 
bank loan it took out to repay the Assembly Republican PAC ("AR-
PAC"). 
You advised me that, generally, if one non-controlled 
committee could use restricted funds for a purpose permitted by 2 
Cal. Code of Regs. section 18536.2, that committee could 
contribute restricted funds to another non-controlled committee 
for a similarly permitted purpose. 
In other words, if PAC A 
could use its restricted funds to retire a debt incurred prior to 
January 1, 1989, then PAC A can contribute restricted funds to 
PAC B to retire PAC B's pre-1989 debt provided the contributions 
are within the applicable contribution limit. 
If this letter correctly reflects the information and 
advice conveyed during our conversation, could you please sign, 
date, and return the copy enclosed and return it to me in the 
envelope provided. 
If any of the understandings recited are 


Mr. John McLean, Counsel 
April 5, 1989 
Page 2 
incorrect, please advise me of that at your earliest convenience. 
I would appreciate a written follow-up in that event as well. 
Thank you, John, for your assistance in this matter. 
JSD:ss 
Enclosure 
cc: Cathy Taylor 
John McLean 
Date 
JUDITH SPROUL DAVIS 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
April 12, 1989 
Judith Sproul Davis 
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson, 
Parrinello & Mueller 
770 L Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Ms. Davis: 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-203 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on April 6, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
Very truly yours, 
-""'\

Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

sections.conclusion: This is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects that advice.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether non-controlled political action committees (PACs) can contribute restricted funds to another non-controlled committee (Project 90) for the purpose of retiring pre-1989 debts, specifically a bank loan.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that if one non-controlled PAC can use restricted funds for a permitted purpose under California Code of Regulations section 18536.2, that PAC can contribute those restricted funds to another non-controlled PAC for a similarly permitted purpose, provided the contributions are within applicable contribution limits.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of three letters: (1) FPPC confirmation letter dated April 14, 1989; (2) incoming request letter from Davis dated April 5, 1989 seeking written confirmation of telephone advice; (3) receipt/acknowledgment letter from FPPC dated April 12, 1989. Primary response is the April 14 confirmation. Question and conclusion are implicit in the correspondence rather than formally sectioned.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether non-controlled political action committees (PACs) can contribute restricted funds to another non-controlled committee (Project 90) for the purpose of retiring pre-1989 debts, specifically a bank loan.

CONCLUSION: This is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects that advice.

embedding.summary: This is a confirmation letter from FPPC General Counsel Diane M. Griffiths to attorney Judith Sproul Davis confirming advice previously provided by telephone on March 30, 1989 regarding the use of restricted PAC funds for contributions to retire Project 90 committee's debt.
